items.title: Looking back at a wild Notre Dame win over UMass that had a funny score
items.summary: By late September of 2015, Notre Dame’s offense was becoming one of the most dangerous units in college football. Whether it was C.J. Prosise breaking long runs, Will Fuller stretching defenses deep, or DeShone Kizer growing more comfortable under center with each passing week, the Irish had discovered something powerful: they could score in bunches. […]

items.title: Notre Dame should join the Texas Tech boycott
